steps.title: Locate zero values on the graph
steps.content: A value of $x$ that satisfies $f(x)=0$ appears where the graph of $y=f(x)$ touches or crosses the $x$-axis.

steps.title: Compare the curve to the axis
steps.content: For $f(x)=ab^x+c$, the graph stays entirely on one side of its horizontal asymptote $y=c$. The shown curve lies below $y=-7$, so the full graph stays below the $x$-axis.

steps.title: Count the intersections
steps.content: Because the graph never reaches $y=0$, there are no values of $x$ for which $f(x)=0$. The answer is D.
